title: Peristylus holochila
intro: Peristylus holochila, also known as the yellow helmet orchid, is a species of orchid native to Australia. It is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing and the treatment of various infections. The bioactive constituents of P. holochila include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, P. holochila is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of respiratory and gastrointestinal issues.
taxonomy: Peristylus holochila is a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae. Within the Orchidaceae family, it is classified under the subfamily Orchidoideae and tribe Diurideae. The species further belongs to the genus Peristylus. This classification places Peristylus holochila within the Orchidaceae family.
